questions.question: What is the approximate percentage of oxygen in expired air?
questions.reason: The percentage of oxygen inspired is 21% and expired is 16% this is because gas exchange occurs in the lungs

questions.question: Nitrogen in expired air by a mammal was found out to be 78%
questions.subtitle: where did this nitrogen in expired air come from?
questions.A: It is a product of proteins broken down in the mammal.
questions.B: It is a product of respiration.
questions.C: It is exchanged for oxygen which is taken into the blood.
questions.D: It is in the air that was breathed in.
questions.reason: The body does not utilize nitrogen gas, so it remains unchanged

questions.question: What helps oxygen to be absorbed rapidly into the blood in the lungs?
questions.A: Air breathed in has less oxygen than air breathed out.
questions.B: Alveoli have thick walls and a large surface area
questions.C: Alveoli have thin walls and a large surface area
questions.D: The concentration of oxygen in the blood is higher than in the alveol
questions.reason: The thin walls and larger surface area of the alveoli allow faster and maximum diffusion of oxygen into the bloodstream

questions.question: A girl holds her breath for 30 seconds, breathes out, and then breathes in.
questions.subtitle: Compared with the air she breathes out, the air she breathes in contains less

questions.question: The diagram shows a section through an alveolus and a capillary.
questions.subtitle: Why does carbon dioxide move from X to Y?
questions.A: Air has a lower concentration of carbon dioxide than blood.
questions.B: Carbon dioxide moves more freely in air than in blood.
questions.D: Diffusion of carbon dioxide can only be out of the blood.
